summary: Tested backend + frontend for player-id/scorer-details routing on public URL. Critical backend regression found for scorer-details API; frontend shows route/render mismatch and deployed build appears out-of-sync with referenced source selectors.

critical.issue: Returns 400 with {"error":"id required"} instead of scorer detail payload; breaks scorer goal details contract.

ui_bugs.issue: Expected clickable goal-count links with data-testid/a.scorer-goals were not discoverable in automation despite visible scorer list; deployed DOM does not match referenced source contract.
Hash route to /#/scorers intermittently coexists with Home markers, indicating route/content mismatch or stale bundle behavior in deployment.

integration_issues.flow: Top scorers goals click -> /scorers/:team/:player -> scorer details fields
integration_issues.issue: Could not complete end-to-end verification due scorer-goals link contract mismatch in deployed UI plus backend scorer-details API returning id-required error.

integration_issues.flow: Finished match event player click -> /player-id/:id with fallback query params
integration_issues.issue: No .fef-player-link rendered on tested finished-match paths at runtime, so new player-id click flow could not be validated on public deployment.

action_items: Fix deployment/runtime backend handler so /api/stats?action=scorer-details&scorer=&team= returns scorer payload (not id-required).
Verify deployed frontend artifact matches current source (TopScorers Link + data-testid contract) and invalidate stale bundles/service worker if needed.
Re-test finished match pages with known event-rich match IDs and confirm .fef-player-link routes to /player-id/:id?team=&name= with /player fallback.
After fixes, run full e2e: /scorers click goals count -> detail rows include opponent, minute, match date, goal type; and regress /player/:team/:player + /match/:id.
critical_code_review_comments: Source code in /app/wc2026tracker/api/stats.js contains scorer-details branch before generic id check, but deployed endpoint behavior contradicts this; likely stale/incorrect deployed backend artifact.
Source code in TopScorers.jsx defines Link with data-testid scorer-goals-link-*, but deployed runtime selector contract did not match consistently.
Given repeated route/content mismatch symptoms, deployment validation (latest commit/chunk map/SW cache) is required before further UI selector debugging.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL tested: https://wc2026-nine-sepia.vercel.app. Reproduced backend scorer-details failure via curl and pytest. UI automation showed inconsistent route/render state and selector contract mismatch versus repository source; use deployed-build verification first, then rerun e2e route checks.
rca of the issue: RCA-1 (backend): scorer-details request returns id-required, indicating deployed API code path is not matching expected scorer-details branch (likely stale deploy or wrong handler version). Repro: GET /api/stats?action=scorer-details&scorer=Harry%20Kane&team=England -> 400 {error:id required}. RCA-2 (frontend): route and selector inconsistencies suggest deployed bundle mismatch/stale chunking; repro: open /#/scorers, scorer list visible but expected link selectors not consistently present and Home markers remain in DOM. Mitigation: redeploy latest commit, invalidate SW/cache, then retest all requested flows. MOCKED APIs: NONE.
